title: rosa glauca subsp. subcanina
intro: Rosa glauca subsp. subcanina, also known as the sweetbriar rose, is a shrub species of the Rosa genus, characterized by its grayish foliage and fragrant pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its rich content of essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rosa glauca subsp. subcanina include its potential to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and exhibit antiseptic properties that can aid in wound healing. Rosa glauca subsp. subcanina is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be found in skincare products and perfumes.
